name: Italy
description: Italy blends world-class education with unparalleled cultural heritage. Home to some of the world's oldest universities, it offers programs in architecture, arts, fashion, and engineering.

tuitionFees.undergraduate: Italian universities charge tuition fees from international students and offer Diploma, Bachelors and Masters programs exclusively taught in English.
tuitionFees.postgraduate: Italian universities charge tuition fees from international students and offer Diploma, Bachelors and Masters programs exclusively taught in English.
tuitionFees.phd: €900–€1,500 per year; often fully funded through research grants

scholarships.name: Students are being called to the Embassy for an interview after which the Visa will be granted within 10 to 15 days

requirements: Study visa (Type D) from Italian consulate

languageRequirements.notes: Italian B2 for Italian programs; growing number of English programs

faqs.question: Is it easy to get a student visa for Italy?
faqs.answer: With proper documentation and financial proof, obtaining a student visa for Italy is straightforward. MVR Consultants can guide you through the entire process.

faqs.question: What are the tuition fees in Italy?
faqs.answer: Tuition fees vary by university and program. Contact our counselors for program-specific fee structures.

faqs.question: Can I work while studying?
faqs.answer: Most countries allow international students to work part-time during term time. Specific hours and conditions depend on the country's immigration rules.

faqs.question: Are there scholarships available for Indian students?
faqs.answer: Yes, many universities and governments offer merit-based and need-based scholarships for international students. Ask our counselors for scholarship matching.
